p.strong: Hardware-Level Context Switching and Latency Hiding

div.p: When Warp 4 hits a high-latency instruction (Memory Read), the Warp Scheduler immediately context-switches to Warp 0 with zero overhead.
Constraint: Register state for all warps (0-31) must reside concurrently in the Register File to allow cycle-accurate switching.

g.text: Peak Throughput: ~15.7 TFLOPS FP32 | Register Bandwidth: 32 TB/s

ul.li: MEM_LATENCY: Waiting for Global Memory access (L2/HBM)
